title: 15 Lead ECG Monitoring
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Suspected posterior myocardial infarction.
- Reciprocal changes in anterior leads (as per CMG).
- To be performed secondary to a 12 lead ECG where there is a high suspicion of acute coronary syndrome but no ECG changes are evident.

#### Procedure

- Explain the procedure to the patient.

- Locate V4R and attach V1 electrode: 
- V4R is located at the 5th intercostal space on the right side of the chest, mid-clavicular line (see **Figure 1**).

- Attach V5 to the V8 position: 
- Posterior 5th intercostal space, mid-scapular region on the left side of the back (see **Figure 1 & 2**).

- Attach V6 to the V9 position: 
- 5th intercostal space, left paraspinal boarder (see **Figure 2**)

- Ensure the patient is as still as possible.

- Print and record the ECG: 
- Ensure that V1 is marked V4R, V5 is marked V8, and V6 is marked V9 on all 15 lead ECGs.

Figure 1

Figure 2
